what_it_does: Implements a React feedback surface for an AI interview/intake UI: a useFeedback provider hook that marks right-click targets, a FeedbackLayer that mounts a context menu, a text/voice capture modal, and a chat modal for discussing a selected turn. It records feedback and persistent learnings to global stores and can trigger live regeneration of the current interview turn.
observations: React hooks are aliased as useStateFb, useEffectFb, useRefFb, and useCallbackFb before use.
The file relies on window globals: React, MS_FEEDBACK, MS_FEEDBACK_ACTIONS, SpeechRecognition/webkitSpeechRecognition, and window.claude.complete.
Feedback targets are attached via an onContextMenu handler and a data-fb-target attribute.
CaptureModal supports three action flavours: raw feedback, persistent learning, and live iteration via interview.redo.
DiscussModal sends the target's private reasoning, briefSnapshot, and contextHint to an external completion API so Vi can explain itself.
Speech recognition is configured with lang 'en-AU', continuous and interim results enabled.
ContextMenu repositions itself if it overflows the viewport after initial render.
Modal overlays close when the overlay itself is clicked.
standouts: DiscussModal exposes the model's private reasoning and snapshot to the operator and to the external API for candid explanation.
CaptureModal can immediately regenerate the current interview turn through interview.redo.
The right-click target pattern couples UI affordances directly to interview turn metadata.
conventions: Global window namespace for shared services and stores.
Inline event handlers and imperative store calls.
Modal close on overlay click and Escape key.
Aliased hook names to avoid collisions or lint rules.
concerns: Aliased React hook names reduce readability for standard React code.
Speech recognition instances are recreated on mic toggle without guaranteed cleanup of the previous instance.
DiscussModal transmits potentially sensitive internal reasoning and snapshot data to an external API.
CaptureModal submit does not reset busy state if store calls or interview.redo throw.
ContextMenu viewport-fit effect only runs on mount, not when menu coordinates change.
No visible validation or schema for the target ref object beyond optional chaining.
teachThis pins the last user message as a learning without confirmation or review.
notes: A compact, global-state-backed feedback layer that lets operators right-click any interview element to complain, teach, regenerate, or interrogate the agent's reasoning.
questions: Where are MS_FEEDBACK, MS_FEEDBACK_ACTIONS, and window.claude.complete defined and documented?
What is the exact shape of the interview object and its redo/current/pinCurrent methods?
How are target refs constructed and what fields are guaranteed to be present?
Are SpeechRecognition permissions and browser support handled elsewhere?
Where are the Icon, Btn, and ViMark components and the fb-* CSS classes defined?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated purpose; it additionally wires captured feedback to persistent learning records and live turn regeneration.
intention_signals: Provide an always-available, in-context feedback channel so operators can correct, understand, and retrain the interview agent while using it.
novelty: Combines right-click target marking, live voice capture, immediate turn regeneration, and a reasoning-exposing chat into a single feedback surface.
contribution: A self-contained feedback surface pattern consisting of a provider hook, a mounted layer, and three coordinated affordances (capture, discuss, teach) wired to global feedback/learning stores and interview redo.
summary_for_embedding: React feedback UI module providing useFeedback hook, FeedbackLayer, context menu, text/voice capture modal, and discuss modal that record feedback and learnings and can regenerate an interview turn.
intention_for_embedding: Enable operators to give in-context feedback, teach persistent preferences, request turn regeneration, and ask the agent to explain its reasoning during an interview.

symbols.does: Renders a positioned right-click menu of feedback actions for a selected target.
symbols.description: ContextMenu receives menu coordinates and target, fetches actions from window.MS_FEEDBACK_ACTIONS.forTarget, groups them by category, and calls onRun when an item is clicked. It also closes on Escape or outside clicks and nudges itself to stay inside the viewport.

symbols.does: Document mousedown handler that closes the context menu on outside clicks.
symbols.description: Checks whether the clicked element is outside the menu ref and invokes onClose; registered in a useEffect in ContextMenu.

symbols.does: Document keydown handler that closes the context menu on Escape.
symbols.description: Listens for the Escape key and invokes onClose; registered alongside the mousedown listener in ContextMenu.

symbols.does: Modal for typing or voice-capturing feedback about a target.
symbols.description: CaptureModal presents a textarea, optional continuous speech recognition, and a submit path that records raw feedback, adds a persistent learning if desc.learn is set, or calls interview.redo if desc.iterate is set. It reports success via a toast message.

symbols.does: Persists captured content and optionally triggers learning or turn regeneration.
symbols.description: Trims the textarea content, writes a feedback record to window.MS_FEEDBACK, adds a learning record when desc.learn is true, and awaits interview.redo when desc.iterate is true. It sets busy state and finishes with a contextual toast.

symbols.does: Chat modal that lets the operator discuss a selected target with Vi.
symbols.description: DiscussModal maintains a message history, builds a rich context string from the target ref including private reasoning and snapshot, sends it to window.claude.complete, and renders the reply. It also offers a 'Teach this' button that pins the last user message as a learning.

symbols.does: Builds a human-readable description of the target element for the discussion prompt.
symbols.description: Collects fields such as question, text, helper, options, scale, optional flag, private reasoning, briefSnapshot, and contextHint into a newline-separated string used as context for the Claude completion.

symbols.does: Appends the operator message and requests a Vi reply via the Claude API.
symbols.description: Validates input, adds a user message, formats the full conversation history plus elementContext into a prompt, awaits window.claude.complete, and appends the returned reply or a fallback error message.

symbols.does: Converts the last user message into a persistent learning and closes the modal.
symbols.description: Finds the most recent user message in the discussion history and calls window.MS_FEEDBACK.addLearning with scope, kind 'prefer', the message text, and contextHint, then invokes onClose.
